title: metrosideros falcata
intro: Metrosideros falcata, also known as the New Zealand lemonwood or lemon-scented paperbark, is a species of flowering plant in the family Myrtaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in Maori medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The leaves and bark of Metrosideros falcata have been used to treat various health issues, such as fever, colds, and skin conditions, due to its health benefits, including antioxidant and antiseptic properties. Herbal preparations of Metrosideros falcata can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
